title: Simandou ships record quantity of iron ore, swelling Chinese stocks
section_path: Simandou ships record quantity of iron ore, swelling Chinese stocks
section_path_text: Simandou ships record quantity of iron ore, swelling Chinese stocks
summary:
Main
Guinean iron ore shipments breach 1.2mt for the first time as Simandou ramps up, with Brazil and Australia also seeing exports increase. China, so far, has absorbed the additional tonnage. Image reference: unnamed.png | assets/2026-05-11_simandou-ships-record-quantity-of-iron-ore-swelling-chinese-stocks_img_unnamed_08c242640370.png
Key Takeaways
● Iron ore flows in April 2026 were up 7% y/y.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Guinean iron ore shipments breach 1.2mt for the first time as Simandou ramps up, with Brazil and Australia also seeing exports increase. China, so far, has absorbed the additional tonnage.

children.title: Guinea to China will dominate the cape market
section_path: Guinea to China will dominate the cape market
children.section_path_text: Guinea to China will dominate the cape market
children.summary: Chinese buying appetite will be the biggest factor in shaping the change in iron flows for the rest of this quarter. With stocks rising to record levels during 2026 already and consumption from steel making weak, a push back on flows is the most likely outcome.
